title: What to know about Centella asiatica before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Centella asiatica are its efficacy in treating memory loss due to its content of asiaticoside, brahmoside, and madecassoside, which have been shown to enhance memory and cognitive function by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters. Centella asiatica also has a significant role in wound healing, attributed to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, which stimulate collagen synthesis and enhance the proliferation of fibroblasts, thereby promoting tissue repair. Furthermore, the plant's antioxidant and anxiolytic properties, largely due to the action of flavonoids and phenolic acids, make it an effective treatment for anxiety and stress-related disorders. Additionally, Centella asiatica has been used to address hair loss by stimulating hair growth and improving scalp health, thanks to the presence of madecassic acid and asiatic acid, which have been shown to enhance the activity of 5-alpha-reductase inhibitors. Centella asiatica has also been used to treat a range of conditions, including diabetes, skin conditions such as eczema, digestive issues, inflammation, and Alzheimer's disease, all of which are attributed to the plant's rich content of triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Centella asiatica are attributed to its antimicrobial effects, which are primarily due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids that inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. Centella asiatica exhibits antioxidant properties, which are mainly attributed to the bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Its anti-inflammatory properties are associated with the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, mediated by compounds like asiatic acid and madecassic acid. Additionally, the neuroprotective effects of Centella asiatica are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins that protect against neurodegenerative diseases by inhibiting amyloid-beta aggregation and oxidative stress. Centella asiatica's wound healing, antiseptic, antiviral, analgesic, hepatoprotective, and cardioprotective properties are also attributed to its bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins, that promote tissue repair, inhibit pathogen growth, and protect against liver and heart damage.
identification: Centella asiatica is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Apiaceae, characterized by its small, greenish-white flowers and umbrella-like inflorescences. It has palmate leaves with 5-7 lobes, typically 2-5 cm in length, and a distinctive leaf shape. Native to tropical and subtropical regions, this plant is widely distributed in Asia, Africa, and Australia, typically growing in shallow water or on wet soil in areas such as ponds, lakes, and marshes. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and can tolerate a wide range of water levels, making it adaptable to various environments. Centella asiatica can grow up to 30 cm in height and is often found in dense groups, forming a dense mat of foliage.
cultivation: Centella asiatica is a low-maintenance, herbaceous perennial plant that thrives in warm, humid climates and can grow up to 30 cm tall, typically spreading by stolons to form dense mats. It requires partial shade to full sun, with an optimal temperature range of 20-30°C, and high humidity, usually above 60%, to promote healthy growth. To cultivate this plant, it's essential to provide well-draining soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0, and ensure adequate water supply, although it can tolerate some drought. Growth can be accelerated by maintaining high humidity, using mulch to retain moisture, and providing supplemental lighting, especially during the initial growth stage. Regular pruning and division can help control its spread and encourage healthy, robust growth.
history: Centella asiatica is an herb that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices in India and the Middle East. The plant, also known as Gotu Kola, has been employed to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and memory loss, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it has been used to promote wound healing and improve cognitive function. Historical records also suggest that it was used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, such as eczema and psoriasis. The plant's bioactive compounds, including asiatic acid and brahmoside, have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic effects.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a class of polyphenolic compounds in Centella asiatica, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: A group of compounds found in Centella asiatica with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are triterpenoid glycosides with hemolytic and surface-active properties, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids found in Centella asiatica are hydroxylated, aromatic compounds with antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Madecassoside
constituents.description: A triterpenoid saponin found in Centella asiatica, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and wound-healing properties.

constituents.name: Asiatic acid
constituents.description: A triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and the ability to improve wound healing and skin regeneration.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Alkaloids found in Centella asiatica are reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides are the constituents in Centella asiatica with triterpenoid saponin properties.

constituents.name: Asiaticoside
constituents.description: A triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Triterpene saponins
constituents.description: Triterpene saponins are a type of bioactive compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, and have been shown to exhibit hepatoprotective, neuroprotective, and antimicrobial effects.

constituents.name: Madecassic acid
constituents.description: A pentacyclic triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties

constituents.name: Tricin
constituents.description: Tricin is a flavonoid constituent of Centella asiatica with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Asiaticoside A
constituents.description: Asiaticoside A is a pentacyclic triterpen saponin with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Asiaticoside B
constituents.description: Asiaticoside B is a triterpenoid saponin with neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins in Centella asiatica are astringent, antiseptic, and have antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: A class of compounds found in Centella asiatica, known for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a class of compounds present in Centella asiatica, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties.

constituents.name: asiaticoside
constituents.description: A triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties

constituents.name: madecassoside
constituents.description: A triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and wound-healing properties.

constituents.name: Asiaticoside C
constituents.description: A triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Centella asiatica exacerbates diabetes and worsens symptoms in patients with autoimmune diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis
Centella asiatica increases the risk of bleeding when combined with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications.
Centella asiatica extracts increase the amplitude of neural signals in the brain, enhancing cognitive function.
Causing irritation, especially in the skin, due to its saponins.
Existing conditions such as epilepsy, asthma, and autoimmune diseases may be exacerbated in individuals taking Centella asiatica extracts.
Centella asiatica induces sedation in high doses.
Hypersensitivity to stimuli in individuals with a history of post-traumatic stress disorder or other anxiety disorders
Centella asiatica potentiates the activity of certain neurotransmitters and hormones.
It has been reported to improve memory and cognitive function in some studies.

uses_list.description: It contains a compound called asiaticoside, which has been shown to improve memory and learning in animal studies by increasing the growth of neurons and enhancing the functioning of the nervous system.

The extract of this plant has been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in diabetic patients, potentially improving blood sugar control and insulin sensitivity.

Studies have shown that compounds present in this plant have the ability to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure in animal models, which may contribute to its potential use as a treatment for hypertension.
The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this plant may help to soothe and calm skin irritation, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing in patients with eczema.
The plant's extract has been found to have anxiolytic effects in animal studies, suggesting that it may be useful in the treatment of anxiety disorders by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
The anti-inflammatory properties of this plant may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in patients with psoriasis, a skin condition characterized by red, scaly patches.

The extract of this plant has been found to improve cognitive function and memory in animal studies by increasing the growth of neurons and enhancing the functioning of the nervous system, which may contribute to its potential use in the treatment of dementia.

The plant's extract has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in patients with conditions characterized by chronic inflammation.

The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this plant may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in patients with osteoarthritis, a condition characterized by joint pain and stiffness.

The extract of this plant has been found to have neuroprotective effects and improve cognitive function in animal studies, which may contribute to its potential use in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease, a progressive neurological disorder that affects memory and cognitive function.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat hypertension by reducing blood pressure through its vasodilatory effect. Additionally, it has been employed to address memory loss by enhancing cognitive function and neuronal plasticity. Furthermore, Centella asiatica has been found to have anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety disorder symptoms by modulating the GABAergic system. Its potential therapeutic applications also extend to diabetes management, where it has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties have made it a valuable treatment option for skin inflammation, eczema, Alzheimer's, Parkinson's disease, hepatitis, wound healing, traumatic brain injury, psoriasis, dementia, stroke, depression, osteoarthritis, gastritis, chronic fatigue, multiple sclerosis, and neurodegenerative diseases, while also promoting liver health and enhancing the body's ability to heal from injury.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat memory loss and dementia by enhancing cognitive function and neuroplasticity. It also helps manage hypertension and stroke by reducing blood pressure and preventing vascular damage. Additionally, Centella asiatica is used to treat diabetes, anxiety, eczema, and psoriasis by regulating blood sugar levels, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the immune response. Furthermore, it is used to alleviate inflammation, stress, fatigue, osteoarthritis, depression, arthritis, skin inflammation, and gastritis by reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines, regulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, and promoting wound healing. Centella asiatica is also used to treat neurodegenerative diseases, including Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and Alzheimer's-related conditions, by protecting neurons and preventing neuroinflammation.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat memory loss due to its antioxidant properties and ability to improve cognitive function. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds also help manage diabetes by reducing oxidative stress and improving insulin sensitivity. Centella asiatica is also applied topically to treat eczema and other skin conditions like psoriasis, which are characterized by inflammation and skin lesions. Additionally, the plant is used to lower hypertension and alleviate anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, while its anti-inflammatory properties also help with digestive issues and inflammation. Furthermore, Centella asiatica is used to prevent hair loss and promote hair growth, as well as to treat neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease, insomnia, dementia, osteoporosis, stroke, and anxiety disorders.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat various health conditions, including memory loss due to its ability to improve cognitive function by enhancing blood flow to the brain. Centella asiatica has also been used to manage diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity, as well as eczema by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Furthermore, it has been employed in the treatment of hypertension, psoriasis, anxiety, and osteoarthritis due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, Centella asiatica is used to address hyperlipidemia, chronic fatigue, skin ulcers, hair loss, osteoporosis, skin infections, alopecia, rheumatism, varicose veins, insomnia, and hemorrhoids, as well as epilepsy and dementia. These therapeutic effects are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as asiatic acid and madecassic acid.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, which it helps to regulate through its antioxidant properties. Additionally, the plant has been shown to improve memory loss by enhancing cognitive function and protecting against neurodegeneration. Centella asiatica also has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating diabetes complications, such as diabetic nephropathy. Furthermore, it has been used to alleviate anxiety, eczema, HIV-related inflammation, dementia, psoriasis, Parkinson's disease, and Alzheimer's disease, as well as stress, rheumatoid arthritis, gastritis, skin conditions, depression, wound healing, gastrointestinal issues, skin inflammation, hyperlipidemia, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and promote tissue repair. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to its efficacy in treating these conditions.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the presence of bacosides, a group of flavonoids that are known to improve memory and cognitive function by protecting the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting the growth of new neurons, and enhancing the communication between neurons.

This plant contains a number of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis, asthma, and other inflammatory diseases.

The bacosides present in this plant are known to improve memory by protecting the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting the growth of new neurons, and enhancing the communication between neurons, which leads to improved memory and cognitive function.

This plant contains a number of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the liver from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, and promote liver health.

The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of this plant help to promote wound healing by reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and enhancing the growth of new skin cells, which leads to faster and more efficient wound healing.
The flavonoids and other bioactive compounds present in this plant have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress, and promote relaxation and calmness.

The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of this plant help to improve skin health by reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and enhancing the growth of new skin cells, which leads to healthier and more youthful-looking skin.

This benefit is due to the presence of bacosides, a group of flavonoids that are known to improve memory and cognitive function by protecting the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting the growth of new neurons, and enhancing the communication between neurons.

The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of this plant help to protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, which leads to improved eye health and reduced risk of age-related macular degeneration and other eye diseases.

The bioactive compounds present in this plant have immunomodulatory effects, which help to boost the immune system and protect against infections, which leads to improved overall health and reduced risk of illness and disease.

benefits_description: Centella asiatica improves cognitive function through its ability to increase blood flow to the brain, and its bioactive compounds, including asiatic acid and brahmoside, have been shown to have neuroprotective effects. Reducing inflammation is another key benefit, as the plant's triterpenoids and saponins have been found to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, leading to a decrease in inflammation and alleviation of related conditions. Enhancing memory is also a notable benefit, with studies demonstrating that Centella asiatica extract increases the expression of genes involved in memory and learning. Protecting the liver is another important property, as the plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, leading to a decrease in liver damage and inflammation. Additionally, promoting wound healing, reducing anxiety, improving skin, enhancing cognitive function, supporting eye health, and boosting the immune system are all benefits attributed to Centella asiatica, due to its ability to stimulate collagen synthesis, reduce stress hormones, protect against UV damage, increase neural plasticity, reduce oxidative stress in the retina, and increase the production of cytokines and immunoglobulins.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Centella asiatica improves circulation by possessing vasodilatory and antioxidant properties, which promote the relaxation of blood vessels and protect against oxidative stress. Enhancing vasodilation allows for the efficient distribution of oxygen and nutrients to tissues, contributing to the boost in blood flow that Centella asiatica provides. This increased blood flow reduces blood pressure by improving the efficiency of the circulatory system, allowing for the enhanced oxygenation of tissues and the stimulation of collagen production, which is essential for maintaining healthy skin and connective tissue. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Centella asiatica reduce inflammation and prevent infection, thereby promoting the improvement of vein health and the enhancement of endothelial function. By supporting cardiac health and reducing inflammation, Centella asiatica also improves the overall efficiency of the circulatory system, ultimately reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Centella asiatica reduce inflammation by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which mitigate the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and suppress the activation of NF-κB, thereby alleviating pain and promoting tissue repair. Stimulate digestion with the help of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria and fungi, and its ability to enhance motility, facilitating the proper movement of food through the digestive system. Soothe irritation caused by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which also boost metabolism by increasing the expression of genes involved in glucose and lipid metabolism. Improve absorption by increasing the expression of transporters involved in nutrient uptake, and regulate bowels by exerting prebiotic properties that promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, thereby preventing ulcers caused by the reduction of gastric acid production. Centella asiatica also enhance its health benefits by regulating the gut-brain axis, which is mediated by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, allowing it to repair tissues and regulate bowels.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Centella asiatica regulates hormones by modulating the expression of genes involved in hormone synthesis and secretion, particularly the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis. This plant stabilizes metabolism through its antioxidant properties, which protect cells from oxidative stress and damage, thereby promoting healthy glucose and lipid metabolism. Centella asiatica enhances thyroid function by increasing the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone and improving the conversion of thyroid hormones, T4 to T3. It supports adrenal function by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the adrenal glands, leading to improved cortisol regulation and reduced fatigue. Additionally, Centella asiatica improves insulin sensitivity, boosts cortisol levels, regenerates the pancreas through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, balances ovarian function by regulating estrogen and progesterone levels, maintains prostate health through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and synchronizes reproductive hormones by regulating the menstrual cycle and improving fertility.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Centella asiatica improves skin barrier through its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which help maintain skin integrity and prevent water loss. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties enhance wound healing by reducing oxidative stress and promoting collagen synthesis, thereby stimulating collagen production. Centella asiatica reduces inflammation quickly due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which also contribute to fighting skin infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogens. Additionally, its antioxidant properties protect against UV rays, reducing the risk of photoaging and improving skin elasticity. Centella asiatica soothes skin irritations and reduces scar formation by promoting tissue repair and regeneration, ultimately boosting the immune system and protecting the integumentary system as a whole.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Centella asiatica boosts lymphatic flow by increasing the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator that relaxes blood vessels and enhances fluid circulation. This plant improves lymph node function by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in these vital organs, thereby facilitating the removal of toxins and waste products. Centella asiatica enhances detoxification process through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and prevent cellular damage, allowing the lymphatic system to efficiently eliminate pathogens and metabolic byproducts. By stimulating lymphatic drainage and activating the lymphatic system, Centella asiatica supports lymphatic tissue health, which is essential for maintaining the integrity of the lymphatic network. Additionally, Centella asiatica regulates lymphatic system function by modulating the activity of various enzymes and hormones, thereby protecting against infection, reducing lymphedema risk, and enhancing interstitial fluid removal, while also stimulating an immune response.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Centella asiatica improves joint mobility by modulating the extracellular matrix, thereby facilitating the movement of joints. This improvement is achieved due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce oxidative stress and prevent damage to the musculoskeletal system. Enhanced bone density is also a result of Centella asiatica's ability to stimulate collagen production and increase bone mineralization, making it a valuable asset for individuals suffering from osteoporosis. Centella asiatica reduces muscle fatigue by increasing the production of ATP and enhancing the efficiency of energy metabolism, allowing for prolonged physical activity. By decreasing inflammation, stimulating collagen production, relaxing muscle tension, speeding up recovery, boosting ligament health, increasing muscle strength, and fostering tendon repair, Centella asiatica plays a crucial role in maintaining musculoskeletal health.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Centella asiatica improves cognitive function by enhancing memory and learning through its neurotrophic and neuroprotective properties, which promote the growth and survival of neurons. Boosting neuroplasticity, Centella asiatica allows the brain to adapt and reorganize in response to new experiences, thereby supporting memory and learning. Enhancing focus, this plant's adaptogenic properties help regulate the body's stress response, thereby reducing anxiety. By regulating neurotransmitters and protecting neurons from oxidative stress, Centella asiatica supports the health of the nervous system. Centella asiatica also fights neurodegeneration by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the expression of antioxidant enzymes, ultimately improving mood and reducing anxiety.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Centella asiatica enhance fertility by increasing sperm count due to its antioxidant properties, which help protect sperm cells from oxidative stress and damage. The plant boost ovulation by regulating hormone balance, which is attributed to its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens that disrupt ovulation. Centella asiatica regulate menstruation by strengthening uterine walls, a result of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and promote healthy uterine tissue. It increase libido by increasing hormone balance, thanks to its antioxidant properties that protect hormones from oxidative damage and support their optimal functioning. Centella asiatica alleviate PMS symptoms by reducing menopausal symptoms, which is a result of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and promote healthy hormonal balance, thereby supporting reproductive health.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Centella asiatica reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues by virtue of its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Improve breathing is facilitated by its antioxidant properties, which scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the lungs. Boost immunity is also enhanced due to its immunomodulatory effects, which stimulate the production of white blood cells and activate immune cells. Relieve congestion is achieved through its expectorant properties, which increase mucus production and facilitate its expulsion from the airways. Calm allergies, kill bacteria, fight infections, lower fever, prevent coughs, and enhance lung function are all attributed to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogens and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Centella asiatica protect bladder from damage with its antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause infections. The antioxidant properties in Centella asiatica prevent UTIs by neutralizing free radicals that can damage the urinary tract and reduce inflammation, thereby preventing damage to the bladder and urethra. Centella asiatica improve kidney function by reducing oxidative stress and promoting the removal of toxins, and its anti-inflammatory properties regulate hormones by reducing inflammation in the endocrine system. The plant's antioxidant properties strengthen urethra and enhance detoxification by reducing oxidative stress and promoting the removal of toxins from the body, which also lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels. Centella asiatica ease urination and promote healing by reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of toxins, thereby reducing the risk of complications and promoting a smooth recovery.

properties_list.description: Centella asiatica has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of triterpenoids and other compounds. These compounds have been found to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, thereby preventing the development of infections.

Centella asiatica contains antioxidants such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been found to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. This property helps to prevent cell damage and may contribute to the plant's potential health benefits.

The triterpenoids present in Centella asiatica have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The anti-inflammatory effects of Centella asiatica may also contribute to its potential benefits for wound healing and skin health.

Centella asiatica has been found to exhibit antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of triterpenoids and other compounds. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria, including Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, thereby preventing the development of infections.

The compounds present in Centella asiatica have been found to exhibit antifungal properties, which may help to prevent the growth of various fungi, including Candida albicans and Aspergillus niger. This property may contribute to the plant's potential benefits for skin health and wound healing.

Centella asiatica has been found to exhibit antiviral properties, which may help to prevent the replication of various viruses, including herpes simplex virus and influenza virus. The antiviral effects of Centella asiatica may contribute to its potential benefits for immune system health and disease prevention.

The compounds present in Centella asiatica have been found to exhibit antihistamine properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as itching and inflammation associated with allergic reactions. This property may contribute to the plant's potential benefits for skin health and immune system function.

Centella asiatica has been found to exhibit neuroprotective properties, which may help to prevent or alleviate conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease. The neuroprotective effects of Centella asiatica may be attributed to the presence of triterpenoids and other compounds that help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

The triterpenoids and other compounds present in Centella asiatica have been found to promote wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing inflammation. This property may contribute to the plant's potential benefits for skin health and wound care.

Centella asiatica has been found to exhibit immunomodulatory properties, which may help to regulate immune system function and prevent or alleviate conditions such as autoimmune disease and cancer. The immunomodulatory effects of Centella asiatica may be attributed to the presence of triterpenoids and other compounds that help to modulate immune cell activity and prevent inflammation.

parts_description: The most used parts of Centella asiatica for medicinal purposes are leaves, roots, and rhizomes. The leaves contain flavonoids, including asiaticoside and madecassoside, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, promoting wound healing and tissue repair. The roots and rhizomes, rich in saponins and triterpenoids, possess immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory activities, also used to treat various skin conditions and wounds. The roots have higher concentrations of asiatic and madecassic acids, contributing to their enhanced anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These bioactive compounds collectively contribute to Centella asiatica's traditional uses in treating various health conditions, including skin wounds, inflammation, and cognitive impairment.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in major bioactive compounds such as asiatic acid, madecassic acid, brahmoside, brahminoside, and centellosides, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, providing primary health benefits including wound healing, improved cognitive function, and protection against neurodegenerative diseases.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain bioactive compounds like brahmoside, brahminoside, and centellosides, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, conferring primary health benefits including anti-aging, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic effects, as well as improved cardiovascular health and immune system function.

parts_list.name: rhizomes
parts_list.description: The rhizomes of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds such as asiatic acid, madecassic acid, brahmoside, and centellosides, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, providing primary health benefits including improved cognitive function, neuroprotection, and enhanced wound healing, as well as anti-aging and anti-cancer effects.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used for treating anxiety, insomnia, memory and cognitive impairments, inflammation, and as a wound healing agent, and to improve skin health, including treating acne and eczema.
preparations.preparation_overview: Centella asiatica capsule is used to treat various conditions, including memory loss, wound healing, anxiety, hyperlipidemia, skin irritation, hypertension, fatigue, and stroke. It's a widely used preparation, found in many herbal supplements and traditional medicines. The effect of taking Centella asiatica capsule is often reported to be improved cognitive function, faster wound healing, and reduced anxiety levels. This preparation is typically made from the leaves and roots of the Centella asiatica plant. The process of making Centella asiatica capsule is moderately complex, requiring a combination of extraction and encapsulation techniques to produce a standardized and effective product.
preparation_recipe: Dry the Centella asiatica leaves and roots in a shaded area to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried leaves and roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent, such as starch or gelatin, to facilitate capsule formation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture of ground Centella asiatica and binding agent.
Seal the capsules securely to prevent leakage and preserve the herbal content.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), and to improve cognitive function and memory.
preparations.preparation_overview: Centella asiatica tincture is commonly used to treat various health issues including wound healing, hemorrhoids, scarring, memory loss, stress, inflammation, high blood pressure, skin rash, sleep disorder, and diabetes. It is a widely used preparation in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian countries like India and China. The preparation has a common effect of promoting overall well-being, likely due to its antioxidant and adaptogenic properties. Centella asiatica tincture is typically made from the leaves and roots of the plant, which are rich in compounds like asiaticoside and madecassoside. The process of making the tincture is moderately difficult, requiring proper extraction and concentration of the plant's active compounds, but the end result is a potent remedy for a range of health conditions.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Centella asiatica leaves and roots, clean them thoroughly, and dry them in a warm, dark place to preserve their potency.
Combine the dried Centella asiatica leaves and roots in a ratio suitable for the desired herbal tincture strength, and coarsely chop them.
Place the chopped Centella asiatica leaves and roots in a clean glass jar, and cover them with a high-proof solvent such as vodka or ethanol.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing the mixture to steep and extract the herbal properties for several weeks.
Strain the Centella asiatica tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool, dark place.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat skin wounds, cuts, and abrasions, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
preparations.preparation_overview: Centella asiatica salve is a topical preparation used to treat various skin conditions, including wound healing, burns, psoriasis, scarring, scar tissue, bruising, eczema, skin irritation, stretch marks, and varicose veins. It's a common herbal remedy, widely used in traditional medicine. The salve's effectiveness is attributed to its ability to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation. Centella asiatica salve is typically made from the leaves and roots of the plant, which contain active compounds like asiatic acid and madecassic acid. However, the preparation's complexity and sensitivity to processing require precise and controlled methods to ensure its quality and efficacy, making it a challenging product to manufacture.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Centella asiatica leaves and roots, and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the leaves and roots in a warm, well-ventilated area, or use a food dehydrator to speed up the process.
Combine the dried Centella asiatica leaves and roots in a large pot, and add enough carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, to cover them completely.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler or a slow cooker over low heat for several hours, or until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discard the solids, and add any desired beeswax or essential oils to create a salve consistency.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to improve cognitive function, reduce inflammation, and accelerate wound healing, and to treat conditions such as anxiety, depression, and skin disorders including eczema and psoriasis.
preparations.preparation_overview: Centella asiatica tea is a herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including wound healing, psoriasis, eczema, anxiety, digestive issues, insomnia, memory loss and high blood pressure. This preparation is widely used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian countries, where it's a staple in many households. One of its most common effects is improved skin health, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Centella asiatica tea is typically made with the leaves and rhizomes of the plant, which are rich in active compounds like triterpenoids and saponins. Preparing this tea is relatively straightforward, requiring only a few minutes of steeping the herbs in hot water.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Centella asiatica leaves and rhizomes from a trusted source.
Wash the leaves and rhizomes thoroughly with clean water to remove any impurities.
Combine the leaves and rhizomes in a large pot and add enough water to cover them.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container and discard the solids, serving the Centella asiatica herbal tea hot or cooled as desired.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is traditionally used to enhance cognitive function, improve wound healing, and reduce inflammation, as well as to treat fever, anxiety, and insomnia.
preparations.preparation_overview: Centella asiatica decoction is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat a range of conditions including hypertension, diabetes, psoriasis, gastritis, Alzheimer's disease, burns, skin ulcers, and menorrhagia. It's one of the most common traditional herbal preparations used worldwide due to its effectiveness and safety. The decoction has a common effect of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Centella asiatica decoction is made by combining leaves and roots of the plant in water and boiling them to create a decoction. The preparation is considered moderately difficult to make as it requires precise measurement of the plant material and adequate boiling time to extract the bioactive compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean a sufficient amount of fresh Centella asiatica leaves and roots.
Combine the leaves and roots in a pot and add enough water to cover the herbs.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for an extended period.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for later use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and depression by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, as well as to improve cognitive function and memory.
preparations.preparation_overview: Centella asiatica essential oil is commonly used to treat eczema, wound healing, psoriasis, skin irritation, muscle pain, hair loss, anxiety, depression, and chronic fatigue. This essential oil is widely used in traditional medicine and is often incorporated into skincare products. Centella asiatica essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its common effect of reducing swelling and promoting tissue repair. The oil is typically extracted from the leaves and roots of the Centella asiatica plant, which grows in tropical regions. The process of extracting essential oil from the plant is moderately difficult, requiring specialized equipment and techniques.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and clean Centella asiatica leaves and roots, ensuring they are free from contaminants.
Dry the harvested leaves and roots in a well-ventilated area or using a drying equipment, such as a dehydrator.
Steam distill the dried leaves and roots to extract their essential oils and volatile compounds.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separatory funnel or decantation, and filter the oil to remove any impurities.
Store the obtained Centella asiatica herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Centella asiatica are exacerbating liver damage, especially in individuals with pre-existing conditions such as hepatitis or cirrhosis, as the plant's active compounds can interact with medications like warfarin and statins, amplifying their effects and potentially leading to adverse outcomes. Furthermore, improperly using Centella asiatica can provoke an allergic reaction in some individuals, characterized by symptoms such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Moreover, Centella asiatica may aggravate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia in individuals with a history of mental health disorders, particularly when consumed in large quantities or combined with other stimulants. Additionally, Centella asiatica may induce gastrointestinal side effects, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, especially in individuals with sensitive stomachs. Improperly using Centella asiatica can also trigger a range of other side effects, including headaches, dizziness, and palpitations, which may be potentiated by other medications or health conditions, thereby enhancing the risk of adverse outcomes, and disrupt the body's natural balance, leading to a range of health issues.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Centella asiatica may interact with certain medications, including blood thinners, diabetes medications, and blood pressure medications.
Use with caution in individuals with hypertension due to potential hypotensive effects.
Centella asiatica may stimulate uterine contractions and inhibit milk production, posing risks to fetal development and infant nourishment.
Prolonged use requires monitoring by a healthcare professional to adjust dosage and manage potential adverse effects.
Individuals with known allergies to the Apiaceae family, such as carrot or celery, should report allergies due to Centella asiatica's similar chemical composition.
Interacting with blood thinners, diabetes medications, and other medications can increase the risk of bleeding and alter blood sugar levels.
Initial doses of Centella asiatica should be limited to 300-400 mg per day to minimize potential gastrointestinal side effects.
Centella asiatica may cause liver damage in people with pre-existing liver conditions, and liver enzymes should be monitored regularly.
Consuming large amounts can cause liver and kidney toxicity.
Centella asiatica should be prepared by decoction or infusion in boiling water to release its bioactive compounds.

intro_study: According to a study published by Phytotherapy research: PTR, Centella asiatica has been found to have a range of positive health benefits, including its ability to demonstrate various pharmacological effects and health benefits. The bioactive compounds present in it have been shown to have therapeutic potentialities, which have been confirmed in many preclinical and clinical studies. Furthermore, its triterpenoids have been found to have a significant impact on various health problems, providing an enriched resource of drug molecules and potential effective drug candidates with reduced side effects.

According to a study published in Frontiers in Pharmacology, Centella asiatica has been found to possess excellent antioxidant properties, as evidenced by its high free radical scavenging activity and reducing capability. Additionally, it has been shown to exhibit anti-hyperlipidemic effects, lowering levels of total cholesterol, triglyceride, and low-density lipoprotein cholesterol while increasing high-density lipoprotein cholesterol. Furthermore, the study suggests that Centella asiatica may be useful in the treatment of oxidative-stress related diseases such as hyperlipidemia due to its ability to protect liver health.

According to a study published by "Contact dermatitis", Centella asiatica has been found to be a very weak sensitizer, making it a low-risk option for frequent application to damaged skin. It has been effectively used in the treatment of various skin conditions, including keloids, leg ulcers, and phlebitis, due to its soothing and healing properties. The treatment of these conditions with Centella asiatica extract is associated with a low risk of acquiring contact sensitivity to the plant or its constituents.

According to a study published by "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", Centella asiatica has been found to possess high antioxidant properties, with its cell suspension culture exhibiting the highest scavenging activity of 79%. This suggests that it may be a valuable plant in traditional medicine for its potential to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, the study's findings provide valuable insights into the phytochemical composition of Centella asiatica, which may have implications for its use in future drug development.

According to a study published by "Metabolites", Centella asiatica has been used to treat various skin diseases, including leprosy, lupus, and eczema, for thousands of years in traditional medicine. It has been shown to display activity in tissue regeneration, cell migration, and wound repair by promoting fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis. This plant's active component, asiatic acid, has been directly associated with its healing activity, contributing to its therapeutic potential in wound healing.

According to a study published by "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Centella asiatica has been found to exhibit anti-tumour effects by inhibiting the proliferation of cancer cells in a dose-dependent manner. The study revealed that the partially purified fraction of Centella asiatica significantly suppressed the multiplication of cancer cells, including Ehrlich ascites tumour cells and Dalton's lymphoma ascites tumour cells, with minimal toxic effects on normal human lymphocytes. Furthermore, oral administration of the extracts from Centella asiatica was shown to retard the development of solid and ascites tumours, ultimately increasing the life span of tumour-bearing mice.

According to a study published by "Zhong yao cai = Zhongyaocai = Journal of Chinese medicinal materials", Centella asiatica has been found to have a significant therapeutic effect on chronic renal failure (CRF) rats, particularly in improving electrolyte levels and hematocrit (HCT) levels. The study revealed that Centella asiatica enema significantly improved red blood cell counts (RBC) and hemoglobin (HGB) content in CRF rats, similar to the positive control group. Overall, the results suggest that Centella asiatica can be an effective treatment option for CRF, with high doses showing the most pronounced benefits.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Centella asiatica are multifaceted, with notable antimicrobial activity attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of various pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, thereby protecting against infections. The plant's antioxidant properties play a crucial role in neutralizing free radicals, which can damage cells and contribute to oxidative stress, a condition linked to chronic diseases. Additionally, Centella asiatica exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which are mediated by the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and the reduction of inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory disorders. Its antibacterial properties are also noteworthy, as they have been shown to be effective against a range of bacterial species, including Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli. Centella asiatica's antifungal and antiviral properties, combined with its antihistamine and neuroprotective effects, enable the plant to modulate the immune system and promote wound healing, while its immunomodulatory properties allow it to regulate immune responses, ultimately contributing to its wide range of therapeutic applications.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Centella asiatica are flavonoids, triterpenoids, saponins, phenolic acids and madecassoside, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Flavonoids, particularly asiaticoside and madecassoside, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities due to their ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes. Triterpenoids, including asiatic and madecassic acids, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Saponins, specifically asiaticoside A and madecassoside, display neuroprotective and anti-apoptotic properties by blocking the activation of caspases and reducing oxidative stress. Madecassoside, a key triterpenoid saponin, has been demonstrated to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, enhancing blood flow and reducing inflammation.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Centella asiatica for medicinal purposes are capsules, which are often used to treat cognitive impairment and memory loss due to their high concentration of asiaticoside. Capsules are usually standardized to contain a specific amount of active compounds, making them a convenient and reliable option for patients. In addition to capsules, a tincture can be used to treat anxiety and insomnia, as it is easily absorbed into the bloodstream and can be taken sublingually. Topical preparations, such as salves, are applied directly to the skin to treat wounds and promote tissue repair, while herbal teas, like those made from Centella asiatica, can be consumed to support digestive health and reduce inflammation. Centella asiatica can also be made into a decoction, which is often used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Centella asiatica medicinally are consulting a doctor before use, especially for individuals with pre-existing medical conditions or taking prescription medications, as it can interact with certain drugs and exacerbate underlying health issues. Monitor blood pressure regularly, as Centella asiatica may lower blood pressure in some individuals, potentially leading to hypotension. Avoid using Centella asiatica in pregnancy and breastfeeding, as there is limited research on its safety during these periods, and potential risks to the fetus or infant cannot be ruled out. Use Centella asiatica under medical supervision, as its effects may be unpredictable and require close monitoring, particularly when combined with other herbs or medications. Report any allergies or adverse reactions to Centella asiatica, and be aware that it may cause interactions with medications, such as blood thinners, and should be started with low doses to minimize potential side effects, while also monitoring liver function and avoiding excessive consumption through proper preparation methods.
intro_description: Centella asiatica, commonly known as gotu kola, is a medicinal herb widely used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, depression, and insomnia, as well as to improve cognitive function and memory. The herb is also employed in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of age-related diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. The main health benefits associated with Centella asiatica include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The herb's bioactive compounds, including asiatic acid, madecassic acid, and brahmoside, have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Centella asiatica is often prepared as a tea or infused into oil to treat a range of ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and neurological disorders.
